title: What To Do When Your Phobia Of Complications Stunts Your Growth.
filename: What To Do When Your Phobia Of Complications Stunts Your Growth..en.vtt
excerpt: Hi everyone and I did a little video last night around what's stopping you in your business and one of the common themes and actually I hear this a lot throughout our organization from people who've been trained and at different stages when you meet people is that fear of complic…

segments.text: hi everyone and I did a little video last night around what's stopping you in your business and one of the common themes and actually I hear this a lot throughout our organization from people who've been trained and at different stages when you meet people is that fear of complications is one of the big limiting factors in building your clinic and that that's actually kind of rightfully so that you have a big fear of if something goes wrong what's that gonna mean for my business and so you think well until I feel sure about that I'm not gonna keep building my business and I'm building a clinic and in many ways that's kind of a good thing and so I wanted to try and break down what you can do to get over a fear of complications in practice because it's not easy but there is a route because some people do it obviously and so there must be a way and the first thing I'd say is you've got to take on board that this fear of complications is not really it's more of a fear of what will happen to you if

there is a complication because none of us linger outside other people's clinics checking capillary refill for that tradition it's not our responsibility so this is all linked with responsibility and the and what will actually happen to you if you don't know what to do and there's a complication and that is linked also massively with us being health care professionals and we know that we are going to be held accountable like it's actually going to affect our life personally if we don't if we are not in a position to properly care for our patients and something happens to our patients so it's kind of a good thing that you feel afraid about complications but the next thing is to try and actually figure out what this anxiety is telling you and what you need to do to move away from it so that you can actually get on and build something that helps your patients and keeps people safe so if you think about wearing white anxiety is it's kind of a fear of something that's in the unknown often so a big part of anxiety the way I understand it is that this there's an unknown quantity like what will happen

if and it's kind of a big question mark and that's kind of what keeps you awake and makes you stop doing thing it's the uncertainty that lies ahead so probably one of the best things you can do about to avoid complication is actually start to break down that uncertainty and actually train and this is hard work it's not this doesn't this takes some mental effort to break this down and decide what to do next but get really clear about where this anxiety is now I talk to patient trainees about this you've been stuck in this situation and it's really interesting the stuff that you get back so for some people it's about being hated like their deepest fear is that they will do something and other people will hate them like it's not even about being sued and losing money it's about being be putting yourself in a position where you end up as this hated figure of this you know this dreadful kind of Botox doctor or Botox nurse who hurts people and takes money from them and you end up a pariah in your neighborhood like that's a really deep down fear and for other people it is about being sued and for someone's about

losing their livelihood like I've come this far and I've got this thing and if I if it fails and someone gets hurt that I'm then I'm gonna lose my livelihood and my children will end up on baked beans you know so it's and that's a real quote from someone who you know quite well he's doing really well and but we all have those things so first of all understand what it is that your anxiety is telling you that you need to guard safely because otherwise it's just anxiety it's like this horrible feeling if you can ask yourself a series of questions so we usually call it like the five why's like you ask us of why five times and you usually get down to a root cause that's actually I'm fundamentally important to your survival or your or it is basically that if the fear if the emotion that you have is so powerful that it's actually making you unhappy like it's life and death like on a level on an emotional level is life and death and now when you break it down you often discover oh it's not actually life and death because now I realize that you know it's unlikely that by having a complication that

my whole neighborhood will hate me you might have you might wish that conclusion you also may not but first of all it's understanding why this is important so really break down the fear what's driving you to not do these treatments what's what the real threat to your existence is and from then you can better decide on how to move forward so the next thing terms of moving forward is to actually explode the different variables is so anxiety a lot about uncertainty complications about uncertainty how do you manage that uncertainty how do you reach a point where you've got enough in your armory of kind of skills and knowledge and experience that you can that you feel like you've got enough certainty to move forward you will never have a hundred percent certainty I don't have a hundred percent certainty something bad can happen in my clinic this afternoon that's never happened before on the face of the earth and I'm not even going to diagnose it because I've never seen it before that is possible it's just incredibly unlikely so I don't feel scared going in and doing my thing this afternoon so break down exactly what is you're afraid

of for mostly this is vascular occlusion for most people so you're thinking if I get a vascular occlusion everyone in my neighborhood is gonna hate me and I'm gonna lose my business if that's the equation the story that you're telling in your head it's very clear why you find that so terrifying the story in my head if I get a vascular occlusion is very different and that's what I want you guys to move towards which is the story in my head is if I get a vascular occlusion I'm going to diagnose it early it'll be a small one because of my injection techniques now I'm going to know how to treat it I'm gonna rescue that patient and then I'm actually going to use that as a teaching experience so that other people get better at aesthetics as well that's exactly what I did with the only occlusion I've had with me injecting and I knew that was going to be the process all the way along so for me I don't get anxiety in the same way because I feel like I'm protected and because of all that I've done to take steps to make myself safe and that's the key thing identify the threat I'm scared of vascular occlusion break down exactly what you do and don't know about vascular occlusion and this is kind of like an audit of what youth and this is where the hard thinking comes on

because you need to literally look every single step oh my god the lip is blue what do you do next if that's not clear in your head you're gonna have uncertainty and you're gonna think I'm just going to fall to pieces you it's almost like you it's okay to be on the edge of uncertainty but you need to at every stage know the most important thing to do so that the outcome is the least uncertain that you can make it so do you know how to rapidly diagnose an occlusion if you don't know the answer to that do start working on that problem that's the first step make sure you would actually I'm actually working a video which we're going to release for free which will help you with this so it's much more structured than this type of video where I'm just talking live to the camera but make sure you can diagnose it and if you can diagnose it what do you do next so it that's your next what if so okay is it as it actually isn't fusion what do I do next if that's not really clear in your head you're gonna have uncertainty you can see this is gonna repeat and repeat so if you do know how to treat it you start off the process of treating it do you know how long that's

going to take this is something that created massive uncertainty in me the in the occlusion I had as it took longer than I thought it took 90 minutes instead of 45 minutes and I went through my realm of comfort into the realm of uncertainty and it felt awful so that's the kind of thing that will lower your phobia you know that if you get an occlusion it's going to probably take 90 minutes to dissolve and you're going to inject high laser you know every 20 minutes with whatever protocol you decide to follow and at the end of that you should reduce the risk of it you should restore blood flow if you haven't what are you going to do next this is it the continuous questioning what are you going to do to decrease uncertainty so I'm going to you know I'm going to get help from an online forum from the beginning so I've got advice from someone or I know the phone number of a doctor who or a experienced nurse practitioner who will help me so you have that in your in what you're going to do next and what if it still doesn't work what if I can't what if you know see you it's or if you can the principle I'm trying to get across as if you can answer every what

if with a practical step your anxiety will turn away will go away and you will feel in control and the more detailed and vivid that is in your mind the better that you feel about it and then when you've got this beautiful map drawn out literally written out protocol stepwise process were you going to call it every point where it doesn't work out so that you have you ultimately feel like you've got a detailed plan that can keep your patients safe and remembering it's going to protect you because that's really what this is all about you've got a plan that's gonna leave you in a position where you can be certain that you're not going to lose your livelihood you're not going to get sued and be hated and be a pariah and end up with your children eating baked beans and then you can you will naturally your anxiety will go away because every time you have an anxious thought you've got an answer for it and actually that is what anxiety is really for it's supposed to be well actually I there's a difference in fear and anxiety so I am still afraid of occlusions every single time I inject I

feel afraid that something bad will happen what I don't have is anxiety because anxiety is a futile loop of what-ifs where there's no answer so the answer to your problem is have an answer to all of your what-ifs and your anxiety turns to fear is not a bad thing if you're a tightrope walk when you're crossing a ravine you need to be afraid of falling but you don't have anxiety because you have control so the answer to all of your fears around is to go in and delve in and get answers and write them down and spell it out and imagine what it feels like and go to courses and you know read books and get involved really good tip is to get involved with online forums where you see people having inclusions actually try and force yourself to think through what you would do instead of just watching the comments and seeing that other people do try and drive the algorithm in your own head so that you're starting to generate answers for each one of those problems that you see and that's going to diminish your anxiety and it's going to diminish your anxiety for the right reason which because you're going to know what the hell to do when something goes wrong because you've done the hard work and you've thought through it and you've

answered every question on your list and you've ended up feeling certain so Tracy is happened to me three times at six years and it's still something to worry about yeah and as I say Tracy no problem worrying about it just use that worry to drive you to action that's what's worth that's what worried is for make it safer we just a board meeting this morning about the future of how we organize skinned beaver training on one of our key strategic goals is to improve safety and even though we do tons of things I don't feel like we're finished at all like there's loads of things I can do to make things safer and sometimes when I see a really nasty occlusion that's my immediate responses I need to get I don't care if we have one in a million I'm gonna be one in ten million all right well one in 100 million I just want to make it safer and that's okay as long as it the crucial thing is with anxieties it stops you doing anything that's bad for everyone because actually you don't actually improve safety because I'll go around the corner and they'll have their treatment and you've got a clinician who really cares about the outcome it doesn't want to cause harm to someone

who's stuck and isn't actually contributing so there's something in it for everyone if you can use a Sun Dried anxiety to drive you to get safer by decreasing the uncertainty by having an answer for every single question that pops into your head a practical stepwise answer to how to get out of that and the anxiety will go away you'll become a really safe practitioner quite quickly if you keep doing that process and that's great for everyone so everyone gets safer you get to build a business you don't have to lie in bed waiting their suffering at night because of your with the what-ifs because you've got an answer for all of them and you've built them into your process and one of them for example of what if I miss it before I go home and let's just I'll just close on this checking capillary refill after every single procedure that you do is a really good way to leave anxiety the door but you have to believe that that's associated with not causing an occlusion so doing it regularly you will start to get a real sense that this is real and one day you'll see an occlusion that seem really obvious to you sometimes you see them on videos on the on Facebook as well you'll get really certain that you can sleep well

at night because you've done your check you've given them a safety net then you know what they'll do if there's a problem and you will feel better and happier and safer and keep building your business so that those are the broad principles of what to do about anxiety and from complications it's to start with a methodical II work your way through and I hope that helps and any questions as always if you write them on the list yeah Tracy says very obvious when it happens I agree the ones I've seen have been pretty obvious and but I hope you work your way through that this would be great if you shared that list if you've got lists of three or four things I'm terrifying you've actually isolated them you've kind of made that vague problem into a very specific problem and then share that on the list that'd be great tag anyone who you think this will be useful too and share it if you think it's useful I really appreciate all that and thank you all very much for watching I will probably see you again tomorrow bye
